title: BLDP 2025 Business Transformation and Margin Recovery Progress
text: Ballard Power Systems demonstrated significant progress in 2025 with margin recovery and business transformation initiatives. Q1 2025 saw total operating expenses decrease 31%, including reductions of 28%, 32%, and 23% in R&D, G&A, and sales/marketing respectively. Q2 2025 total operating expenses decreased 12% with 30% reduction when excluding restructuring charges. Q3 2025 achieved 40% reduction in cash operating costs year-over-year and 36% decrease in total operating expenses (55% reduction excluding restructuring costs). Full-year 2025 operating expenses are projected between $100-120 million, a 30% reduction from 2024 levels. Revenue growth turned positive with full-year 2025 revenue exceeding $99 million, up 43% YoY. Gross margin improved to 5% for full-year 2025 compared to negative 32% in 2024. Q4 2025 gross margin reached 17%, demonstrating strong operational leverage. Cash operating cost reductions of 41% in Q4 2025 vs Q4 2024 reflect benefits of restructuring. The transformation strategy focused on disciplined spending, commercial term improvements, product cost reductions, fleet services, and expanded market reach, positioning the company toward cash flow breakeven by end of 2027.
